generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BatteryActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator component for battery management systems enabling real-time power distribution and charging adjustments via edge computing-based Digital Twin control"
]

instance SatelliteThrustActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuator system for satellite trajectory adjustments to avoid orbital debris collisions, integrated with real-time Digital Twin trajectory optimization services"
]

instance ManufacturingRobotArm : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial robot arm with closed-loop control capabilities enabled by Digital Twin feedback for precision assembly operations"
]

instance ProductionLineConveyor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Automated conveyor system optimized through Digital Twin process optimization services for enhanced production line efficiency"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency vibration sensor for monitoring mechanical health of manufacturing equipment and automotive components"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Precision temperature sensor for battery systems and manufacturing processes with real-time telemetry capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Pressure monitoring sensor for critical system components in automotive and aerospace applications"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTransmitted
]

instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "3D LiDAR sensor for autonomous vehicle environmental perception and point cloud data acquisition at 10-100Hz frequency"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
]

instance SatelliteSubsystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Space-based sensor suite for tracking satellite subsystem health and orbital debris proximity in aerospace Digital Twins"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SatelliteHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Industrial IoT sensor for monitoring energy consumption across manufacturing facilities and production lines"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "On-board sensor system capturing vehicle dynamics parameters including speed, acceleration, and orientation for autonomous systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance SatelliteHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SatelliteSubsystemSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

instance VehicleStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance PointCloudClusteringDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudProcessingFunctionalEntity
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BatteryDegradationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-informed machine learning model for predicting battery cell degradation and time-to-failure in automotive applications"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BatterySimulationEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingProcessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI-driven digital twin model for optimizing manufacturing workflows and supply chain logistics using reinforcement learning"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessOptimizationEnabler
]

instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity simulation model for vehicle dynamics including control system design and autonomous driving scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutonomousControlEnabler
]

instance SpaceDebrisCollisionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Orbital mechanics simulation model for predicting and avoiding collisions with space debris in satellite systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AerospaceOptimizationEnabler
]

instance PointCloudProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computer vision model for clustering and tracking LiDAR point clouds into structured object representations for autonomous systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutonomousPerceptionEnabler
]

instance VibrationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time vibration data from manufacturing equipment and automotive components used for predictive maintenance analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VibrationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ConditionMonitoringEnabler
]

    base:desc "Continuous temperature monitoring data from battery systems and industrial processes for health monitoring and energy optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationEnabler
]

instance PressureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Pressure monitoring data from critical system components used for operational analytics and safety assessments"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PressureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "3D point cloud data from LiDAR sensors processed into structured object tracks for autonomous vehicle perception"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AutonomousPerceptionEnabler
]

    DTDFVocab:fromData SatelliteHealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SpaceHealthMonitoringEnabler
]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers

    base:desc "Machine learning-based anomaly detection system for predictive maintenance in battery systems and manufacturing equipment"

]

]

]

]

    DTDFVocab:enables ResourceAllocationService
]

instance AutonomousPerceptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

]

    base:desc "Real-time control system for robotic systems, assembly lines, and vehicle dynamics using DT-driven feedback loops"
    DTDFVocab:enables AutonomousControlService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D visualization platform using VR/AR for design review, training, and troubleshooting"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

    DTDFVocab:enables BatterySimulationService
]

instance AerospaceOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

    DTDFVocab:enables AerospaceOptimizationService
]

    base:desc "Space-based Digital Twin system for tracking satellite subsystem health and orbital debris proximity"
    DTDFVocab:enables SpaceHealthMonitoringService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based inter-node communication framework for coordinating Digital Twin functional entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance DataPreProcessingFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Functional entity executing real-time preprocessing of sensor data including point cloud clustering and data validation"
]

    base:desc "Data translation service handling unit conversions and serialization for heterogeneous data streams"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService
]

// C6: Services
instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service using machine learning to forecast time-to-failure for mechanical components and electrical systems"

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS facilitates closed-loop control by translating digital commands into physical actuation. **Control flows** originate from the **Controlling FE**, which interfaces with the **Actuation FE** to execute commands on the physical system. Key components include:

**Command Types**:
- **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Commands**: Adjust vehicle speed and acceleration based on digital twin projections (e.g., object tracks, path planning).
- **Actuation Signals**: Direct motor/steering servo commands derived from control algorithms (e.g., PID or model predictive control).

**Control Message Formats**:
- **Data Translation FE**: Serializes commands into standardized formats (e.g., ROS2 topics or UDP payloads) for inter-entity communication.
- **Message Structure**: Likely includes:
  - **Header**: Timestamp, source ID, and message type (e.g., `ACC_Speed_Adjust`).
  - **Payload**: Numeric values (e.g., target speed in km/h, steering angle in degrees) and metadata (e.g., confidence scores for path projections).

**Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
- **No explicit mention of authentication** (e.g., cryptographic signatures, TLS) in the documents.
- **Data Validation**: Implicit via **unit conversions** and **message alignment** in the Data Translation FE to ensure commands are physically feasible (e.g., servo angle limits).

**Feedback Systems**:
- **Closed-Loop Control**: The **Actuation FE** receives feedback from physical sensors (e.g., motor/servo encoders, vehicle state sensors) and feeds it back into the **Controlling FE** for real-time adjustments.
- **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly detailed, but likely handled via **priority-based scheduling** or **time-sensitive networking (TSN)** mechanisms (implied by real-time requirements).

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- **ACK/NACK Mechanisms**: Not described, but assumed to exist for critical commands (e.g., emergency braking) via **UDP acknowledgments** or ROS2 feedback topics.
- **Execution Logging**: Commands are likely logged in the **Reporting FE** for post-processing analysis.

**Security Protocols**:
- **No explicit mention of encryption** or **access control** for control signals in the documents.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to stricter vehicular application standards despite hardware limitations. Temporal granularity is dictated by the ROS2-based architecture, where each Function Entity (FE) executes as an independent ROS2 package with nodes handling real-time data processing (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). Latency requirements for data pre-processing, actuation, and device communication are implicitly managed by the *Physical Twin Entity*, which interfaces directly with sensors/actuators. No explicit mention of service-level time scales (e.g., per-FE latency targets) is provided beyond the 100 ms synchronization constraint."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an independent ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. This enables modular orchestration, with the *User Entity* (UI components), *Digital Twin Entity* (data/control logic), and *Physical Twin Entity* (resource-specific operations) deployed on separate hardware. Resource management is implicit, leveraging the entity-based structure to partition responsibilities: the *Physical Twin Entity* hosts both resource-specific (e.g., sensors/actuators) and general automotive functions (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). System-wide coordination is achieved via ROS2’s publish-subscribe model, though no explicit mention of higher-level orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, edge orchestration) or cross-entity conflict resolution mechanisms is provided. The design aligns loosely with ISO 23247’s Reference Architecture (RA) but introduces deviations (e.g., expanded *Physical Twin Entity* scope) to accommodate vehicular-specific requirements."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **ISO 23247** standard implicitly addresses fidelity and validity through its **entity-based reference architecture**, which mandates that **Digital Twin Entities (DTEs)** must accurately represent **Observable Manufacturing Elements (OMEs)**. While the standard does not prescribe explicit validation methods, it implies that **uncertainty quantification** and **error detection** are inherent to the system’s design, as each entity (e.g., Data Collection, Digital Twin, User) must ensure consistency between physical and digital representations.

The standard does not specify **formal verification processes**, **uncertainty sources**, or **quality assurance protocols** beyond the requirement for **functional alignment** between entities. However, the **modularity** of Functional Entities (FEs) suggests that validation could be achieved through **interoperability testing** and **data consistency checks** at entity boundaries. No quantitative metrics (e.g., accuracy thresholds, sampling frequencies) or correction mechanisms (e.g., machine learning-based error mitigation) are detailed in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System’s Technical Connection** relies on a **multi-layered network architecture** designed for real-time, low-latency communication. Key details include:

**Communication Protocols**:
- **Intra-System (ROS2)**: Software components (e.g., Data Pre-Processing FE, Controlling FE) communicate via **ROS2**, a middleware framework optimized for robotics and real-time systems. Topics and services are used for message passing.
- **Inter-Entity (Wi-Fi)**: Physical-to-Digital and Digital-to-Physical communication occurs over **Wi-Fi (UDP)** at the transport layer, enabling lightweight, broadcast-capable data exchange between entities (e.g., vehicle sensors ↔ Digital Twin Entity).

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Star Topology**: Centralized Digital Twin Entity acts as a hub, receiving data from peripheral Physical Twin Entities (e.g., vehicle sensors) and distributing commands.
- **Hierarchical Layers**:
  1. **Sensor Layer**: Raw data acquisition (LiDAR, motors, steering).
  2. **Processing Layer**: ROS2-based SW components (Data Pre-Processing, Controlling, Visualization).
  3. **Application Layer**: User interfaces (3D Visualization, Control Panel) and reporting systems.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- **Not explicitly quantified**, but implied to be stringent for autonomous driving:
  - **LiDAR Data**: ~10–100Hz with point cloud sizes in the order of **MB per scan** (depending on resolution).
  - **Control Signals**: Sub-10ms latency for critical commands (e.g., emergency braking).
- **UDP’s lack of reliability** is mitigated by **application-layer retransmissions** or **ROS2 QoS policies** (e.g., history depth, reliability settings).

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., failover nodes, dual Wi-Fi links) is mentioned in the documents.
- **ROS2 QoS Policies**: Likely configured for **best-effort delivery** with configurable history buffers to handle occasional packet loss.
- **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a **single point of failure** for serialization/deserialization; no redundancy is implied.

**Security Protocols**:
- **Transport Layer**: UDP (unencrypted) for inter-entity communication.
- **No mention of**:
  - **Encryption** (e.g., TLS for Wi-Fi links).
  - **Authentication** (e.g., mutual TLS, digital signatures).
  - **Firewall/Network Segmentation** to isolate critical control channels.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **edge-centric**, prioritizing low-latency processing for real-time applications. Key details include:

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Edge Deployment**: Primary processing occurs on **on-premise/edge devices** (e.g., vehicle onboard computers or dedicated Digital Twin servers) to minimize cloud latency.
- **No explicit mention of cloud hosting**, but **Data Storage** and **Reporting FE** could leverage cloud services for archival or analytics (not detailed in documents).

**Computational and Storage Requirements**:
- **Computational**:
  - **Data Pre-Processing FE**: Requires **GPU acceleration** for point cloud clustering/tracking (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or similar).
  - **Controlling FE**: Real-time control algorithms (e.g., ACC) demand **multi-core CPUs** with low-latency I/O (e.g., Intel Xeon or ARM Cortex-A).
  - **Visualization FE**: **GPU-accelerated rendering** for 3D environments (e.g., Unity/Unreal Engine integration).
- **Storage**:
  - **Raw Data**: LiDAR point clouds and sensor logs stored temporarily on **edge storage** (e.g., NVMe SSDs).
  - **Processed Data**: Object tracks, reports, and visualizations stored in **structured databases** (e.g., PostgreSQL or custom NoSQL) via the **Reporting FE**.

**Scalability Mechanisms**:
- **Horizontal Scaling**: Not explicitly supported; system appears **monolithic** with centralized Digital Twin Entity.
- **Modularity**: SW components (e.g., Data Pre-Processing, Controlling) are **ROS2-based**, allowing potential **microservices deployment** if scaled horizontally.
- **No mention of containerization** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) or **serverless** architectures.

**Deployment Models Supported**:
- **On-Premise/Edge**: Primary model for real-time processing (e.g., vehicle-mounted systems).
- **Hybrid**: Possible **cloud-edge synergy** for non-critical data (e.g., historical reports), but not detailed.

**Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
- **High Availability**: Implied via **real-time processing** and **closed-loop control**, but no redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover, load balancing) are described.
- **Fault Tolerance**:
  - **No explicit recovery mechanisms** for component failures (e.g., crashed Controlling FE).
  - **Data Translation FE** is critical; its failure would halt all inter-entity communication.
- **Redundancy**: None mentioned for sensors, network links, or processing units.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The document explicitly references the **ISO 23247:2021 standard** for Digital Twins in manufacturing, which includes:
    - **Four parts**: Overview, Reference Architecture (RA), Digital Representation of Physical Elements, and Information Exchange.
    - **Entity-based RA**: Defines roles for OMEs, Data Collection/Device Control Entities, Digital Twin Entities, and User Entities, with **Functional Entities (FEs)** as modular components.
    - **Interoperability**: Implies adherence to standards like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for interfacing (though not mandatory).
    - **Compliance frameworks**: The standard itself serves as a framework for DT development, but no additional certification requirements or industry-specific standards (e.g., IEC, IEEE) are mentioned.
    - **Information exchange**: Part 4 of the standard likely addresses data formats or protocols, though specifics are not detailed in the provided text.

    The document does not mention **certification requirements** or broader **interoperability standards** beyond OPC UA as an example.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **ISO 26262-1:2018** functional safety standards for **road vehicles**, mandating a structured approach to **cybersecurity and safety-critical system considerations**. Key security measures include:
- **Cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **ISO 26262-1:2018**, emphasizing **risk assessment** through **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level)** decomposition to mitigate threats in **safety-critical systems** (e.g., adaptive cruise control systems referenced in **ISO 15622:2018**).
- **Access control mechanisms** and **authentication systems** are implied for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, though explicit details on granular access policies or cryptographic standards (e.g., TLS, ECC) are not provided.
- **Fail-safe mechanisms** are mandated for **safety-critical functions**, requiring **redundancy, fault tolerance, and deterministic response times** to ensure system integrity under **cyber-physical attacks** or hardware failures.
- **Threat mitigation strategies** are inferred from **ISO 26262-1:2018**, which mandates **systematic risk analysis** (e.g., **FMEA, FTA**) to identify vulnerabilities in **digital twin components** (e.g., real-time simulation models, sensor data feeds).
- **Safety-critical system considerations** are explicitly tied to **automotive safety standards**, where **digital twin cockpits** (as per **Dalibor et al., 2020**) must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** for **safety validation**, including **hardware/software partitioning** and **safety mechanisms** for **interactive simulations**.
- **No explicit mention of data ownership, privacy protection (e.g., GDPR compliance, anonymization techniques), consent management, or data sharing agreements** is found in the provided documents.
"
]
